data.body:
[G4 container review — parent 4fcd, child a36b `worm-folly-barge` | sibling cross-check: 22bf, 5a6c] One finding (mode: scope overlap). The other 6 cross-child modes read clean.

FINDING — Scope overlap (this child a36b ⟷ sibling 22bf `jerky-vista-amok`)
- criteria: parent AC1 ("a mechanism detects material drift in the code the review was grounded against — e.g. the resolved edit-set / cited files"); parent AC3 ("tested: code drift after a signed review → attestation no longer valid").
- location: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` `claim_gate_check` step-3 (whole-HEAD exact-match staleness check). a36b and 22bf both target this exact code site with the same remedy and overlapping test scenarios.
- evidence:
  - a36b prescribes implementation ACs: AC1 "Claim freshness is scoped to the code the review was grounded against (resolved edit-set / cited files) rather than the whole HEAD sha, so an unrelated commit does NOT stale the attestation"; AC2 "A relevant change (to the cited/edit-set files) STILL invalidates"; test AC "review at X, commit an unrelated file, claim succeeds; review at X, commit a cited file, claim is blocked."
  - Sibling 22bf prescribes the SAME remedy and explicitly claims to fix this ticket: description "Content-addressing makes this … immune to … shared-checkout HEAD moves — the specific `worm-folly-barge` complaint"; AC2 "Claim SUCCEEDS when an unrelated file changed after signing (fixes `worm-folly-barge`); claim is BLOCKED when a dependency file's content changed or was deleted"; test AC "review at X, commit an unrelated file → claim succeeds; commit a dependency file → claim blocked."
  - Both edit `claim_gate_check` step-3 and carry near-verbatim overlapping test scenarios (unrelated-file → succeeds; cited/dependency-file → blocked). a36b is currently independently open (status: open) with prescriptive implementation ACs, not a closed reproduction folded into 22bf. The two are linked only by a non-directional `relates_to` edge, leaving ownership of the fix + tests ambiguous.
  - a36b's own context paragraph already concedes the shared root cause and that "The mechanism in this epic's AC1 … would fix both facets at once," reinforcing that the implementing work belongs in one ticket (22bf), not duplicated here.
- scenarios: (a) Both a36b and 22bf get independently claimed/implemented → two competing edits to `claim_gate_check` step-3 and duplicate/conflicting test suites for the same unrelated-file-succeeds / cited-file-blocked behavior. (b) a36b is implemented as a narrower whole-HEAD tweak that diverges from 22bf's content-addressed `{path: content-hash}` design → inconsistent fixes for one root cause.
- impact: Duplicate or conflicting implementation of one behavior across two open sibling tickets; ambiguous ownership of the `worm-folly-barge` fix and its tests; risk of regressing 22bf's under-invalidation goal if a36b is fixed in isolation.
- checklist_item: scope overlap.
- suggested_fix: Make exactly one ticket own the scoped-freshness implementation + tests. Either (i) convert a36b into a non-implementing bug/acceptance witness that 22bf "fixes" (fold its repro + unrelated-file/cited-file scenarios into 22bf's test AC), or (ii) replace the `relates_to` edge with a directional `fixes`/blocking link a36b→22bf and strip a36b's prescriptive implementation ACs. Either way the `claim_gate_check` step-3 change and its test scenarios live in one place (22bf).

The other 6 modes (high-confidence, benign-reading filter applied):
- Implicit shared state — CLEAN w.r.t. a36b: a36b is a bug report and does not itself write into the signed `attest.py` manifest; the manifest-schema sharing concern is between 22bf and 5a6c, not this child.
- Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN: a36b's premise (binding is whole-HEAD, too coarse) matches 22bf's premise and the live code (`claim_gate_check` step-3 exact-match; `material_fingerprint` binds declared `file_impact`, not on-disk content). a36b's AC2 explicitly preserves the under-invalidation goal ("relevant change STILL invalidates"), consistent with the epic. No contradiction.
- Dependency gap — CLEAN-ish: a36b declares `relates_to` 22bf, so an edge exists; its weakness (non-directional) is captured under the scope-overlap finding above rather than as a separate dependency-gap finding.
- Ordering violation — CLEAN: no sequencing constraint between a36b (the reported symptom) and the stories; 22bf (Story 1) carries the fix, 5a6c (Story 2) is unrelated to a36b.
- Consumer impact — CLEAN: a36b prescribes no change to the gate's read contract; the sole consumer (`src/rebar/_commands/claim.py`) reads `ok`/`reason`, unaffected by a verdict-internal scoping change.
- Residual references — CLEAN: a36b introduces no orphaned references; it cites `head_sha`/HEAD-binding only to describe the current behavior it wants changed.
